from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about celestial bodies into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Simple predicate‑to‑sentence templates
    templates = {
        "apoapsis": "{sub} has an apoapsis of {obj}",
        "periapsis": "{sub} has a periapsis of {obj}",
        "orbitalPeriod": "{sub} has an orbital period of {obj}",
        "epoch": "{sub} was recorded at epoch {obj}",
        "discoverer": "{sub} was discovered by {obj}",
        "discovered": "{sub} was discovered on {obj}",
        "mass": "{sub} has a mass of {obj}",
        "averageSpeed": "{sub} travels at an average speed of {obj}",
        "escapeVelocity": "{sub} has an escape velocity of {obj}",
        "temperature": "{sub} has a temperature of {obj}",
        "maximumTemperature": "{sub} reaches a maximum temperature of {obj}",
        "minimumTemperature": "{sub} reaches a minimum temperature of {obj}",
        "meanTemperature": "{sub} has a mean temperature of {obj}",
        "density": "{sub} has a density of {obj}",
        "rotationPeriod": "{sub} rotates every {obj}",
        "surfaceArea": "{sub} has a surface area of {obj}",
        "absoluteMagnitude": "{sub} has an absolute magnitude of {obj}",
        "formerName": "{sub} was formerly known as {obj}",
        "deathCause": "{sub} died of {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "{sub} died in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "{sub} died on {obj}",
        "birthDate": "{sub} was born on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{sub} was born in {obj}",
        "nationality": "{sub} was a {obj} national",
        "stateOfOrigin": "{sub} originated from {obj}",
        "almaMater": "{sub} studied at {obj}",
        "doctoralStudent": "{sub} supervised {obj}",
    }

    # Build clause list, keeping subject only for the first clause to avoid repetition
    clauses = []
    for idx, t in enumerate(triples):
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            full_phrase = tmpl.format(sub=t.subject, obj=t.object)
        else:
            full_phrase = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
        if idx == 0:
            # keep the full phrase for the first clause
            clauses.append(full_phrase)
        else:
            # remove the leading subject (and following space) from subsequent clauses
            sub_prefix = t.subject + " "
            if full_phrase.startswith(sub_prefix):
                clause = full_phrase[len(sub_prefix):]
            else:
                clause = full_phrase
            clauses.append(clause)

    # Assemble a single complex sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure a trailing period
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
